name: Fried Shrimp Won Ton Soup with Smoked Ham & C
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Light soy sauce, or to taste
1/3 cup Cornstarch mixed with 1/3 cup water
2 Green onions, trimmed and finely minced
2 tbsp Minced Smithfield ham or prosciutto
Peanut or corn oil for deep-frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat a wok or deep saucepan over medium-high heat until hot. Add at least 2 inches of oil; heat to 365°F.
2. Deep-fry the wontons in batches, without crowding and turning occasionally, for 2 to 3 minutes or until golden brown.
3. Remove with a skimmer, drain on paper towels. Place the wontons in a shallow 3-quart soup tureen.
4. Bring chicken stock, soy sauce, sherry, and white pepper to a boil in a large stockpot. Stir in the cornstarch mixture and cook until the soup thickens, about 15 seconds. Fold in sesame oil. Remove from heat.
5. Lightly beat the egg whites just to break them up slightly. Stir the soup in a circular motion while slowly pouring in the egg whites. The whites should set into a lacy pattern within seconds.
6. Pour the soup over the wontons, garnish with chopped green onions, cilantro, and Smithfield ham. Serve hot.
